questions.question: What is TLS?
questions.description: TLS is the successor of SSL and protects data in transit.

questions.question: Which TLS feature helps verify the server identity?
questions.description: The server presents a certificate that chains to a trusted certificate authority.

questions.question: Which TLS version should generally be avoided in modern systems?
questions.description: TLS 1.0 is deprecated and should not be used for modern secure communication.

questions.question: What does perfect forward secrecy protect against?
options: Past sessions being decrypted after long-term key compromise
questions.description: Forward secrecy keeps prior session keys safe even if a long-term private key is later exposed.
